description: Deferred Questions (Decisions tab) — displays non-blocking human-in-the-loop questions that were deferred during autonomous workflow execution. Users can review the AI's auto-decisions, approve them, or reject them (triggering targeted rework). Also covers the floating panel on the Active Dashboard and the badge counter on active run cards.

groups.name: Tab Registration & Navigation
groups.description: The Decisions tab appears in the RunDetailContent tab bar and is accessible via the ?tab=decisions URL parameter.

assertions.description: A 'Decisions' tab trigger with a MessageSquareWarning icon exists in the tab bar.
Clicking the Decisions tab switches to the deferred questions content. The tab value is 'decisions' and it renders DeferredQuestionsTab inside a Suspense boundary.
While the DeferredQuestionsTab is loading (lazy import), a fallback with a spinning Loader2 icon and 'Loading...' text is shown.
assertions.precondition: Decisions tab is selected and component is still loading

groups.name: Empty State — No Deferred Questions
groups.description: When a workflow run has no deferred questions (either because blocking_approval was true, or confidence was always high enough to auto-approve silently), an empty state is displayed.

assertions.description: A CheckCircle2 icon (opacity-40, h-8 w-8) is displayed centered in the empty state.
assertions.precondition: No deferred questions exist for this run

assertions.description: The text 'No deferred questions for this run' is shown as the primary empty state message.

criteria.textContains: No deferred questions for this run

assertions.precondition: No deferred questions exist for this run

assertions.description: A secondary description reads 'Questions appear when the system makes autonomous decisions with low confidence'.
assertions.precondition: No deferred questions exist for this run

groups.name: Header — Stats Badges & Bulk Actions
groups.description: When questions exist, a header shows the title, status count badges, and a bulk approve button.

assertions.description: The heading 'Deferred Questions' is displayed at the top of the tab.
assertions.precondition: At least one deferred question exists

assertions.description: A badge showing 'N pending' appears when there are pending questions. Uses the 'default' badge variant with text-xs styling.
assertions.precondition: At least one pending question exists

assertions.description: A badge showing 'N approved' appears when there are approved questions. Uses the 'secondary' badge variant.
assertions.precondition: At least one approved question exists

assertions.description: A badge showing 'N rejected' appears when there are rejected questions. Uses the 'destructive' badge variant.
assertions.precondition: At least one rejected question exists

assertions.description: An 'Approve all (N)' button appears when more than one pending question exists. Clicking it sends a bulk review request to approve all pending questions.
assertions.precondition: More than one pending question exists

groups.name: Question Card — Data Display
groups.description: Each deferred question is rendered as a bordered card showing iteration number, risk level badge, confidence percentage, and the question text.

assertions.description: Each question card displays 'Iteration N' where N is the iteration when the question was raised.
assertions.precondition: At least one deferred question exists

assertions.description: Each question card shows a risk level badge. 'irreversible' and 'high' use destructive (red) variant, 'medium' uses default, 'low' uses secondary.
assertions.precondition: At least one deferred question exists

assertions.description: Each question card shows confidence as a percentage (e.g., '45% confidence'). The value is Math.round(confidence * 100).
assertions.precondition: At least one deferred question exists

assertions.description: The question text is displayed with text-text-secondary color and is limited to 2 lines (line-clamp-2). It should be clearly legible against the bg-surface-base card background.
assertions.precondition: At least one deferred question exists

groups.name: Question Card — Approve & Reject Actions
groups.description: Pending questions show Approve and Reject buttons. Approved/rejected questions show a status badge instead.

assertions.description: Pending questions have an 'Approve' button (outline variant, sm size). Clicking it submits an approval review via POST and shows toast 'Decision approved'.
assertions.precondition: A pending deferred question exists

assertions.description: Pending questions have a 'Reject' button (destructive variant, sm size). Clicking it prompts 'Why was this decision wrong?', then submits a rejection review. Toast shows 'Decision rejected' or 'Decision rejected — rework triggered' if a rework run was created.
assertions.precondition: A pending deferred question exists

assertions.description: Questions that have been reviewed show a status badge ('approved' with secondary variant, or 'rejected' with destructive variant) instead of action buttons.
assertions.precondition: At least one reviewed (non-pending) question exists

groups.name: Question Card — Expanded Details
groups.description: Clicking the chevron expands a question card to show auto-decision details, summary, files modified, review comment, git checkpoint, and timestamps.

assertions.description: Clicking the chevron (ChevronRight when collapsed, ChevronDown when expanded) toggles the expanded details section. Only one question can be expanded at a time.
assertions.precondition: At least one deferred question exists

assertions.description: When expanded, shows 'Auto-decision:' label followed by 'Continued as planned' (if auto_decision_type is 'proceeded') or the auto_decision_detail text.
assertions.precondition: A question card is expanded

assertions.description: When expanded and context contains files_modified, a 'Files modified:' section displays a bulleted list of file paths.
assertions.precondition: A question card is expanded and has files_modified in context

assertions.description: When expanded and git_checkpoint exists, shows 'Git checkpoint: <abbreviated-hash>' where the hash is truncated to 8 characters.
assertions.precondition: A question card is expanded and has a git_checkpoint

assertions.description: When expanded, shows 'Created: <localized datetime>' and optionally '· Reviewed: <localized datetime>' if the question has been reviewed.
assertions.precondition: A question card is expanded

groups.name: Question Grouping — Status Sections
groups.description: Questions are displayed in three sections: Pending Review (with MessageSquareWarning icon), Rejected (with XCircle icon), and Approved (with CheckCircle2 icon). Each section only renders when it has questions.

assertions.description: When pending questions exist, a 'Pending Review' section header with MessageSquareWarning icon is shown above the pending question cards.
assertions.precondition: At least one pending question exists

assertions.description: When rejected questions exist, a 'Rejected' section header with XCircle icon is shown.
assertions.precondition: At least one rejected question exists

assertions.description: When approved questions exist, an 'Approved' section header with CheckCircle2 icon is shown.
assertions.precondition: At least one approved question exists

assertions.description: Sections are displayed in order: Pending Review first, then Rejected, then Approved. This ensures actionable items are always at the top.
assertions.precondition: Questions with multiple statuses exist

groups.name: Visual Design & Styling
groups.description: Design tokens, colors, and typography for the deferred questions UI.

assertions.description: Question cards use bg-surface-base background with hover:bg-surface-raised transition, rounded-lg border, and p-4 padding.
assertions.precondition: At least one deferred question exists

assertions.description: Section headers ('Pending Review', 'Rejected', 'Approved') use text-text-muted color at text-xs size with font-medium weight. They should be visually subtle but legible.
assertions.precondition: At least one section header is visible

assertions.description: Risk level badges follow the color scheme: 'irreversible' and 'high' → destructive (red), 'medium' → default (neutral), 'low' → secondary (muted). This provides immediate visual risk indication.
assertions.precondition: At least one deferred question exists

groups.name: Floating Panel — Active Dashboard
groups.description: During active workflow execution, a floating panel appears in the bottom-right corner showing pending deferred questions. Only visible when pending questions exist and not dismissed.

assertions.description: The floating panel is positioned at bottom-20 right-4 (fixed, z-50) with w-80 width, rounded-lg, shadow-xl, and backdrop-blur-lg. It overlays the active dashboard content.
assertions.precondition: Active dashboard is showing a running workflow with pending deferred questions

assertions.description: The panel header shows a MessageSquareWarning icon (amber-400) and text 'N pending decision(s)' where N is the count. Clicking the header toggles expanded/collapsed state.
assertions.precondition: Floating panel is visible

assertions.description: An X button in the panel header dismisses the panel. The panel reappears when a new deferred question event arrives.
assertions.precondition: Floating panel is visible

assertions.description: The floating panel is not rendered when there are zero pending questions or when the panel has been dismissed.
assertions.precondition: No pending deferred questions exist for the active run

assertions.description: When expanded, each pending question shows compact Approve (outline, h-6) and Reject (destructive, h-6) buttons at text-[10px] size.
assertions.precondition: Floating panel is expanded and has pending questions

groups.name: Floating Panel — Real-Time Events & Toasts
groups.description: The floating panel subscribes to WebSocket events for live updates and shows toast notifications when new questions arrive.

assertions.description: When a 'deferred-question-created' WebSocket event arrives for the current run, a toast.info notification appears with text 'Decision needed ({risk} risk, {confidence}% confidence)' and a description showing the first 120 chars of the question. Duration is 8 seconds.
assertions.precondition: Active dashboard is showing a running workflow

assertions.description: After dismissing the panel, if a new deferred-question-created event arrives, the panel reappears (dismissed state is reset to false).
assertions.precondition: Panel was dismissed and a new question event arrives

assertions.description: When a 'deferred-question-reviewed' event arrives, the question list is refreshed to reflect the updated status.
assertions.precondition: Floating panel is visible and a review event arrives

groups.name: Active Runs Bar — Pending Decisions Badge
groups.description: The CompactRunCard in the ActiveRunsBar shows an amber badge with the count of pending deferred questions, fetched via useEventTriggeredFetch.

assertions.description: When a running workflow has pending deferred questions, an amber badge appears on its card in the active runs bar. The badge shows the count (e.g., '2') with a MessageSquareWarning icon (size-2). Uses text-amber-400 and border-amber-500/20 styling.
assertions.precondition: Multiple active runs exist, at least one has pending deferred questions

assertions.description: When a running workflow has zero pending deferred questions, no amber decisions badge is shown on its card.
assertions.precondition: Active workflow has no pending deferred questions

assertions.description: The badge count updates when a 'deferred-question-created' event arrives (via useEventTriggeredFetch with 30s fallback polling).
assertions.precondition: Active runs bar is visible

groups.name: Data Flow — Dual Source & Sync
groups.description: The Decisions tab fetches data from the runner (live) with fallback to the backend (synced). This ensures deferred questions are viewable even when the runner is offline.

assertions.description: Data is fetched first from the runner via runnerFetch(/task-runs/{id}/deferred-questions). If the runner is available, its data is used.
assertions.precondition: Runner is connected and tab is active

assertions.description: If the runner fetch fails (offline), data is fetched from the backend via httpClient.get(/api/v1/task-runs/{id}/deferred-questions). Questions synced to the backend are displayed.
assertions.precondition: Runner is offline and backend has synced deferred questions
